title: SUV owner experiences a serious – and not uncommon – engine issue
author: John Paul
bullets: A 2018 Hyundai Tucson owner needed a complete engine replacement at 60,000 miles, covered by the 100,000-mile warranty.
Hyundai, Kia, Toyota, and Chevrolet face engine issues due to manufacturing defects, leading to recalls and power loss incidents.
Hyundai/Kia models suffer from improperly machined bearings causing engine knock, limp mode, and potential engine block damage.

title: After running 19 marathons, he’s making the 2026 Boston Marathon his 20th
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Harry Cicma, a Boston resident, will run his 20th marathon at the 2026 Boston Marathon.
Cicma previously ran the Boston Marathon in 2023, one of his most memorable experiences.
He is running to support the Stepping Strong Charity and Brigham and Women's Hospital.

title: Is a Stefon Diggs reunion on the table for the Patriots? Here’s what Eliot Wolf said.
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Patriots executive Eliot Wolf leaves open possibility of re-signing Stefon Diggs, released earlier this month.
Stefon Diggs's cap hit was set to rise from $10.5 million to $26.5 million, prompting his release.
Diggs, turning 33 in November, had 1,013 receiving yards last season despite offseason ACL surgery.

title: Barnstable man arrested on child porn charges
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Scott Grella, 38, from Barnstable, was arrested for possession and dissemination of child pornography.
Police arrested Grella after an investigation linked his online account to child pornography activities.
Grella is held on a $3,000 bail and is scheduled for a pre-trial hearing on May 12.

title: Eliot Wolf refutes report that suggested Tom Brady may have kept Patriots from getting Maxx Crosby
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Patriots VP Eliot Wolf dismissed claims that Tom Brady influenced a trade keeping Maxx Crosby away from New England.
A failed trade attempt sent Crosby to the Ravens due to Baltimore's concerns over the player's medical status.
Brady, now a minority Raiders owner, was rumored to prevent Crosby's move to Patriots, a claim Wolf finds unlikely.

title: 5 best new restaurant openings and dining updates in Boston (March 12, 2026)
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Avra Estiatorio, a Greek restaurant from New York, is set to open in Back Bay, seating 250 people.
Celine, inspired by Montreal cuisine, will open in Seaport in late summer, featuring a top-notch bar program.
The Wonder Food Halls expand in Massachusetts, now open in Natick and other areas, with more locations opening this year.

title: Baystate Health doctor on probation after lewd behavior inside his office
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Dr. Robert A. Martin received a five-year probation for masturbating in his Baystate Medical Center office.
Massachusetts Board of Registration in Medicine stayed his license suspension after Martin agreed to probation terms.
Martin resumed practicing in fall 2024 and has complied with a behavioral health monitoring contract since May 2024.

title: A brilliant example of biophilic design lists on Martha’s Vineyard for $22,950,000
author: Regina Cole
bullets: Oceanfront estate Minawetu in West Tisbury, Martha's Vineyard, lists for $22.95 million by LandVest.
Minawetu features biophilic design with materials like Chelmsford granite, African mahogany, and solar-powered sustainability.
The property includes 8,200 sq ft main house, 7 bedrooms, rooftop pool, and 250 feet of sandy beach access.

title: Woman injured in Natick during marketplace meet-up to sell $27,000 diamond
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: A Natick Mall meet-up for a $27,000 diamond sale ended violently with the suspect allegedly dragging and biting people.
Keyjuan Webb, 23, charged with robbery and assault after attempting to flee with the diamond, threatening bystanders at the scene.
Victim Kathleen Sanfacon suffered injuries and was hospitalized; the diamond is in police evidence, and Webb remains in custody.

title: He’s running Boston to support a family friend’s fight against cancer
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Sean Pruneau, 32, is running the Boston Marathon for Dana-Farber in support of Dennis Ford's cancer fight.
Dennis Ford, a former Jimmy Fund charity organizer, was diagnosed with glioblastoma in August 2023.
Team Dennis has raised over $2.5 million for cancer research, embodying the motto "Fueled by Positive Energy."
